steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: This is a transition question. The first sentence says researchers wondered if rapid spread was due to aggressive behavior; the second reveals that the spiders are actually gentle and freeze under disturbance. The relationship is a reversal of the initial hypothesis.

steps.title: Reason toward the answer
steps.content: Instead signals that what was found contradicts what was expected — precisely the "expected aggression / found gentleness" reversal in the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Therefore,: signals a cause-effect conclusion, but the second sentence contradicts rather than follows from the hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice C — For example,: signals illustration of the same idea, but gentleness isn't an example of aggression — it's the opposite. ✗
  • Choice D — In other words,: signals restatement of the same idea, but the second sentence presents the opposite finding, not a paraphrase. ✗
That is the correct answer.
